content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms from ICM/RND curious agents can be repurposed to prioritize which new experiences are encoded into an autonomous agent’s long-term memory, creating a self-sustaining active memory system that outperforms 2024 SOTA fixed-priority retention rules. This curiosity-aligned memory pruning automatically prioritizes storing high-information, out-of-distribution experiences that drive further active learning, rather than retaining redundant or low-novelty data.
